title: One-line falsifiable claim being tested
hypothesis: Pre-registered, falsifiable statement. Name the single primary metric and the direction of the expected effect. Everything else in the report is secondary/exploratory.
rationale: Why this is worth testing — cite the telemetry observation that motivated it (waterfall gate, audit finding, regret report, journal lesson).
decision_rule: Pre-registered rule the human reviewer applies, phrased against interval bounds, e.g.: 'Advance to shadow (P2) only if the near-miss band's hit-rate CI lower bound exceeds the below band's upper bound AND the accepted-band mean excess move remains positive.' The runner never applies this rule itself.
guardrails: Acceptance volume stays within the 5-15 accepted dry-run decisions/week band
No never-loosen gate fires more often than its pre-experiment baseline
never_loosen_ack: This experiment does not propose changing any control on the CALIBRATION_PLAN never-loosen list; if it did, it requires explicit human approval regardless of results.
